definition: Cap-weighted and equal-weighted indices mask fundamentally different cycle states. The aggregate index obscures the cycle position of the median company: earnings revision breadth is positive for mega-caps but negative for small-caps, and equal-weighted S&P 500 may already show positive stock-bond correlation while cap-weighted shows negative.
evidence: Earnings revision breadth bifurcation between mega-cap and small-cap
Equal-weighted vs cap-weighted correlation regime divergence
Small-cap effective earnings recession since mid-2023
Cap-weighted vs equal-weighted ERP dispersion of ~200bp
open_questions: Whether median company recession eventually propagates to index level
Whether cap-weighted or equal-weighted ERP is the relevant metric
